description: The decline of Orkut, Google's dominant social network in Brazil, and the subsequent takeover by Facebook between 2011 and 2012. This is a classic case study of a platform losing a localized market lead.

key_events.description: Facebook introduces Portuguese language support and optimizes for lower-bandwidth connections in Brazil.
Facebook exceeds Orkut in monthly unique visitors in Brazil (Dec 2011).
Google announces it will shut down Orkut entirely, marking the end of the migration.

calibration_notes: Useful for understanding how global tech giants leverage localization to displace regional incumbents.
